Agreement binding on Republic
SCHEDULE
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K28.00 each.
Kazungula Bridge Authority [No. 12 of 2024 119
GOVERNMENT OF ZAMBIA
ACT
No. 12 of 2024
Date of Assent: 16th August, 2024
An Act to give effect to the Agreement relating to the
establishment of the Kazungula Bridge Authority made
between the Government of the Republic of Zambia and
the Government of the Republic of Botswana; and provide
for matters connected with, or incidental to, the foregoing.
[16th August, 2024
ENACTED by the Parliament of Zambia.
Enactment

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Kazungula Bridge Authority Short title
sections.text:
This Act may be cited as the Kazungula Bridge Authority Short title
Act, 2024.

sections.heading: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text:
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
“Agreement” means the Agreement between the
Government of the Republic of Zambia and the
Government of the Republic of Botswana relating to
the establishment of the Kazungula Bridge Authority,
which is set out in the Schedule;
“Council” means the Council of Ministers constituted under
Article 5 of the Agreement; and
“Kazungula Bridge Authority” means the Kazungula Bridge
Authority established under Article 3 of the Agreement.

sections.heading: (1) The Kazungula Bridge Authority is a body corporate Kazungula
This Agreement governs the matters relating to the establishment and operations of the
sections.text:
This Agreement governs the matters relating to the establishment and operations of the
Authority.

sections.heading: The objectives of the Authority shall be to exercise Contracting Parties= rights of ownership
sections.text:
The objectives of the Authority shall be to exercise Contracting Parties= rights of ownership
of the Bridge Infrastructure and to advise the Contracting Parties on the most appropriate
arrangements to ensure that the Bridge functions as an effective link between their respective
transport systems.

sections.heading: The Authority shall maintain the Bridge Infrastructure and promote its usage in accordance
sections.text:
The Authority shall maintain the Bridge Infrastructure and promote its usage in accordance
with the provisions of this Agreement.

sections.heading: The Authority shall manage and operate the Bridge Infrastructure.
sections.text:
The Authority shall manage and operate the Bridge Infrastructure.
ARTICLE 3 - ESTABLISHMENT

sections.heading: There is hereby established the Kazungula Bridge Authority, an International Organisation
sections.text:
There is hereby established the Kazungula Bridge Authority, an International Organisation
with a common seal, capable of suing and being sued in its name and with powers subject to the
provisions of this Agreement of doing all such acts and things as a juridical person may do or
perform.

sections.heading: The Authority shall be located in Botswana.
sections.text:
The Authority shall be located in Botswana.
Kazungula Bridge Authority No. 12 of 2024 123
ARTICLE 4 - FUNCTIONS OF THE AUTHORITY

sections.heading: The functions of the Authority shall be to—
Subject to the approval by the Council, the Authority may delegate or appoint an agency
sections.text:
Subject to the approval by the Council, the Authority may delegate or appoint an agency
or service provider to collect Tolls and any other charges.
ARTICLE 5 - THE COUNCIL

sections.heading: There shall be a Council of Ministers which shall act as a supervisory body for the
sections.text:
There shall be a Council of Ministers which shall act as a supervisory body for the
Authority.

sections.heading: The Council shall consist of six (6) members, three (3) of whom shall be the Ministers
sections.text:
The Council shall consist of six (6) members, three (3) of whom shall be the Ministers
responsible for finance, trade, transport or infrastructure in the Republic of Botswana, and three
(3) Ministers responsible for finance, trade, transport or infrastructure in the Republic of Zambia,
respectively.

sections.heading: The Council shall meet once annually in ordinary session and may meet in extraordinary
sections.text:
The Council shall meet once annually in ordinary session and may meet in extraordinary
session at the request of either one or both of the ContractingParties or the Board. The meetings
of the Council shall be hosted on a rotational basis between the Contracting Parties.

sections.heading: The Council shall at its annual ordinary session appoint a chairperson and vice chairperson,
sections.text:
The Council shall at its annual ordinary session appoint a chairperson and vice chairperson,
who shall be from the same country, to preside over the affairs of the Council. The chairpersonship
and vice chairpersonship shall be held alternately by ministers of the Contracting Parties.

sections.heading: A quorum shall be formed when at least four (4) members of the Council are present,
sections.text:
A quorum shall be formed when at least four (4) members of the Council are present,
provided that at least two (2) members shall come from each Contracting Party.

sections.heading: The Authority shall provide secretarial services to the Council.
sections.text:
The Authority shall provide secretarial services to the Council.
124 No. 12 of 2024 Kazungula Bridge Authority

sections.heading: The decisions of the Council shall be taken by consensus.
sections.text: The decisions of the Council shall be taken by consensus.

sections.heading: The Council shall regulate its own procedure.
sections.text: The Council shall regulate its own procedure.

sections.heading: The powers of the Council shall include—
The Council may, as it deems necessary, delegate any of its powers to the Board. Such
sections.text:
The Council may, as it deems necessary, delegate any of its powers to the Board. Such
delegation shall outline the specific power delegated and the time period.
ARTICLE 6 - BOARD OF DIRECTORS

sections.heading: The Authority shall have a Board of Directors. The Contracting Parties shall appoint to
sections.text:
The Authority shall have a Board of Directors. The Contracting Parties shall appoint to
the Board an equal number of members. The Board shall consist of ten (10) members as follows:
(a) a permanent secretary from each Contracting Party in the ministry responsible for
infrastructure, transport, works and supply or maintenance;
(b) two (2) members from each Contracting Party with training or experience in transport,
engineering, finance, trade or any other technical qualifications or experience relevant
to the running of the affairs of the Authority;
(c) a representative from the Lead Agency of each of the Contracting Parties; and
(d) the Executive Director and Board Secretary who shall be ex-officio members.

sections.heading: The members of the Board shall be appointed by the respective Contracting Parties
sections.text:
The members of the Board shall be appointed by the respective Contracting Parties
taking into consideration that the members listed in Article 6 sub-article 1(b) do not share expertise
in the same field. Preference shall be given to candidates with expertise in more than one of the
listed fields.

sections.heading: The members referred to in Article 6 sub-article 1(a) shall rotate as Board chairperson at
sections.text:
The members referred to in Article 6 sub-article 1(a) shall rotate as Board chairperson at
the beginning of every financial year. The Board chairperson shall regularly update the Council
regarding the actions taken by the Board. If deemed necessary by the Board or requested by the
Council, the Board chairperson shall be present at the meetings of the Council.

sections.heading: A Board member, except a member referred to under Article 6 sub-article 1(a), shall hold
sections.text:
A Board member, except a member referred to under Article 6 sub-article 1(a), shall hold
office for a period of three (3) years and may be re-appointedfor a further and final term of three
(3) years.
Kazungula Bridge Authority [No. 12 of 2024 125

sections.heading: Subject to this Agreement, the Board shall regulate its own procedure, and shall meet for
sections.text:
Subject to this Agreement, the Board shall regulate its own procedure, and shall meet for
the transaction of its business at least once every three (3) months.

sections.heading: The Board shall make every effort to adopt a decision on the basis ofconsensus. If all
sections.text:
The Board shall make every effort to adopt a decision on the basis ofconsensus. If all
efforts to reach consensus on a matter at a meeting of the Board have been exhausted and no
agreement is reached, the matter shall be referred to the Council for resolution.

sections.heading: Five (5) members, with at least two (2) from each Contracting Party, shall form a quorum.
sections.text: Five (5) members, with at least two (2) from each Contracting Party, shall form a quorum.

sections.heading: The functions of the Board shall be to—
The Board may recommend for approval by the Council, By-laws for the better exercise
Each Board member shall be paid out of the funds of the Authority such sitting allowance
sections.text:
Each Board member shall be paid out of the funds of the Authority such sitting allowance
as the Council may determine.
A RTICLE 7 - APPOINTMENT AND FUNCTIONS OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR

sections.heading: There shall be an Executive Director of the Authority who shall, subject to the approval
sections.text:
There shall be an Executive Director of the Authority who shall, subject to the approval
of the Council, be appointed by the Board and shall be a Zambian national.

sections.heading: The Executive Director shall be responsible for the day-to-day administration of the
sections.text:
The Executive Director shall be responsible for the day-to-day administration of the
Authority.
ARTICLE 8 - HOST AGREEMENT
The Host Party shall be Botswana and Botswana shall conclude a Host Agreement with the
Authority which will stipulate the privileges and immunities to be accorded to the Authority by
the Host Party, as well as other hosting conditions.
ARTICLE 9 - FINANCIAL PROVISIONS

sections.heading: A Fund shall be established which will be administered by the Authority. The Fund shall
sections.text:
A Fund shall be established which will be administered by the Authority. The Fund shall
be made up of all revenue, after payment of the agreed costs and the operational expenditure of the
Authority.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties agree that the monies in the Fund shall only be used—
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties agree that the monies in the Fund shall only be used—
(a) if, in a given financial year, the revenue is insufficient to cover the fees and agreed costs
and the operational expenditure of the Authority;
(b) to fund additional capital expenditure related to the Bridge Infrastructure; and
(c) to invest monies that are not immediately required for the purposes of the Fund in a
manner authorised by the Council.

sections.heading: Unutilised monies in the Fund shall not be disbursed to the Contracting Parties, except in
sections.text:
Unutilised monies in the Fund shall not be disbursed to the Contracting Parties, except in
response to a decision of the Council. This should include a financial forecast of the costs and
income of the Authority for the subsequent three (3) years, demonstrating that distribution of
money to the Contracting Parties is economically justified.

sections.heading: (1) The revenue of the Authority shall consist of—
The accounting currency for the Authority shall be United States Dollar.
sections.text:
The accounting currency for the Authority shall be United States Dollar.
Kazungula Bridge Authority [No. 12 of 2024 127

sections.heading: The financial year of the Authority shall be a period of twelve (12) months from 1st
sections.text:
The financial year of the Authority shall be a period of twelve (12) months from 1st
January to 31st December of each calendar year.

sections.heading: The Board shall, prior to the commencement of each financial year, cause to be prepared
sections.text:
The Board shall, prior to the commencement of each financial year, cause to be prepared
and submitted for the approval of the Council, a budget of the Authority for such financial year.

sections.heading: The Board shall ensure that accounts and other records in relation thereto are kept by the
sections.text:
The Board shall ensure that accounts and other records in relation thereto are kept by the
Authority and shall prepare financial statements in respect of each financial year of the Authority.

sections.heading: The accounts of the Authority in respect of each financial year shall, in accordance with
sections.text:
The accounts of the Authority in respect of each financial year shall, in accordance with
auditing principles consistently applied, be audited by such independent auditors as the Board
may, subject to the approval of the Council, appoint for that purpose.

sections.heading: (1) As soon as the accounts of the Authority have been prepared and audited, which
sections.text:
(1) As soon as the accounts of the Authority have been prepared and audited, which
should not be later than six (6) months after the end of each financial year, the Board shall submit
to the Council an annual report which shall consist of—
(a) an income and expenditure account, a balance sheet, the report of the auditors to the
Authority and details of any directions which may have been given to the Authority
by the Council in terms of this Agreement; and
(b) a statement of capital expenditure certified as correct by the auditors to the Authority
and showing the general heads of such expenditure compared with the figures
therefore in any budget approved for that financial year together with variations;
Provided that if the Authority cannot submit the accounts within six (6) months after
the end of any financial year, the Board may inform the Council of the reason.
(2) The Authority may subject to the approval of the Council and subject to such terms and
conditions as the Council may impose in respect thereof, establish and administer specific reserves
of funds for the purposes of carrying out its functions or for the benefit of its employees.

sections.heading: The Minister responsible for transport or infrastructure shall lay a copy of each annual
sections.text:
The Minister responsible for transport or infrastructure shall lay a copy of each annual
report of the Authority before the National Assembly within thirty (30) days after receiving it.
Provided that, if by reason of the propagation or dissolution of Parliament, this section
cannot be complied with within the stipulated timeframe, theMinister shall lay the annual
report before the Nationals Assembly within thirty (30) days after the date on which the National
Assembly first meets.

sections.heading: The costs associated with the management, maintenance of the Bridge Infrastructure and
sections.text:
The costs associated with the management, maintenance of the Bridge Infrastructure and
operations of the Authority shall be covered from the revenue of the Authority.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties undertake to make good any shortfall of finances of the Authority
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties undertake to make good any shortfall of finances of the Authority
through equal contributions to the Authority as and when required.

sections.heading: The Private Actors shall pay rentals to the Authority. Details outlining the payment of
sections.text:
The Private Actors shall pay rentals to the Authority. Details outlining the payment of
the said fees are to be included in the service level agreements.

sections.heading: In case extra facilities are needed to be constructed on the Bridge Infrastructure, the
sections.text:
In case extra facilities are needed to be constructed on the Bridge Infrastructure, the
Authority and the requesting agent shall negotiate in good faith the terms of use for such facility
in a separate contract, with regard to the extra expenses incurred by the Authority.
128 No. 12 of 2024 Kazungula Bridge Authority

sections.heading: The Authority shall, except as provided in this Agreement, be exempt from the provisions
sections.text:
The Authority shall, except as provided in this Agreement, be exempt from the provisions
of any legislation of the Contracting Parties regarding the audit and control of public accounts, and
in particular exempt from provisions in such legislation regarding the—
(a) payment of surplus revenue to the Consolidated Revenue Funds or the equivalent
funds of the Contracting States;
(b) submission of capital budgets;
(c) submission of revenue and expenditure budgets; and
(d) submission of annual reports and accounts.

sections.heading: The Authority may, on the approval of the Council, retain a proportion of excess revenue
sections.text:
The Authority may, on the approval of the Council, retain a proportion of excess revenue
for contingency purposes and the excess declared as revenue for the Contracting Parties.
A RTICLE 10 - LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

sections.heading: None of the Contracting Parties shall be liable for any acts, omissions or obligations
sections.text:
None of the Contracting Parties shall be liable for any acts, omissions or obligations
ensuing from contracts or other agreements that the Authority has concluded for performance of
activities relating to the Bridge or Bridge Infrastructure, unless such act, omission or obligation
stems from a decision or order directly given to the Authority by the Council.

sections.heading: The Authority shall be responsible for any insurance coverage as may be necessary to
sections.text:
The Authority shall be responsible for any insurance coverage as may be necessary to
protect itself and/or the Contracting Parties against claims or liabilities arising out of the
performance of this Agreement.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties undertake to protect the Authority, its Board members, employees
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties undertake to protect the Authority, its Board members, employees
and agents from any civil or criminal liability in respect of anything done in good faith and without
negligence pursuant to the provisions of this Agreement, save that such protection from liability
shall not have the effect of preventing any person from obtaining redress in a court of competent
jurisdiction for any breach of contract.
ARTICLE 11 - OBLIGATIONS OF THE CONTRACTING PARTIES

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties shall give their full cooperation and support to the implementation
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties shall give their full cooperation and support to the implementation
of this Agreement by having regard to the principle ofgood governance.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties agree, in their respective territories, to construct, maintain and
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties agree, in their respective territories, to construct, maintain and
improve the connecting transport corridor to maximise utilisation of the Bridge Infrastructure.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties agree to take the necessary means to ensure the provision of
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties agree to take the necessary means to ensure the provision of
adequate utility services at the Bridge Infrastructure.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties agree to take any necessary measures including any amendment
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties agree to take any necessary measures including any amendment
of any domestic law required to give effect to the provisions of this Agreement.
ARTICLE 12 - TAX STATUS
The Authority shall be regarded as performing essential governmental functions and to that
extent, the Contracting Parties shall determine the tax exemptions to be accorded to the Authority.
Kazungula Bridge Authority [No. 12 of 2024 129
ARTICLE 13 - DISPUTE SETTLEMENT

sections.heading: Any dispute that may arise in the interpretation, application and implementation of this
sections.text:
Any dispute that may arise in the interpretation, application and implementation of this
Agreement and any supplementary agreements shall be resolved by the Contracting Parties
amicably and in the spirit of friendship and cooperation. This amicable settlement shall be done
by the Council.

sections.heading: In the event that the Council is unable to resolve the dispute within twenty-one (21)
sections.text:
In the event that the Council is unable to resolve the dispute within twenty-one (21)
days, the matter shall be referred to the Heads of State of the Contracting Parties.

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties hereby acknowledge that in resolving such disputes they shall
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties hereby acknowledge that in resolving such disputes they shall
primarily be guided by the need to give effect to the paramount objectives of this Agreement.

sections.heading: Any dispute between the Contracting Parties in terms of this Agreement that remains
sections.text:
Any dispute between the Contracting Parties in terms of this Agreement that remains
unresolved for a period of more than thirty (30) days following efforts at amicable settlement,
shall be referred to the Southern African Development Community (SADC) Panel of Elders for
mediation.

sections.heading: In all instances that the aforesaid dispute is not resolved in mediation, the matter shall be
sections.text:
In all instances that the aforesaid dispute is not resolved in mediation, the matter shall be
referred to an arbitrator(s) appointed by the Contracting Parties. The decision of such arbitrator(s)
shall be binding on the Contracting Parties.

sections.heading: The rules governing such arbitration shall be the United Nations Commission on
sections.text:
The rules governing such arbitration shall be the United Nations Commission on
International Trade Law (UNCITRAL) Rules of Arbitration.

sections.heading: The cost of arbitration under this Article shall be borne equally by the Contracting
sections.text:
The cost of arbitration under this Article shall be borne equally by the Contracting
Parties.
ARTICLE 14 - LANGUAGE
The working language of the Authority shall be English.
ARTICLE 15 - CONFIDENTIALITY

sections.heading: The Contracting Parties shall keep confidential any information in their possession
sections.text:
The Contracting Parties shall keep confidential any information in their possession
received from the other Contracting Party in the course of theimplementation of this Agreement.
Such information shall not be disclosed by the receiving Contracting Party without the prior
written approval of the disclosing Contracting Party or as required by law.

sections.heading: The obligations set out in this Article shall subsist notwithstanding the termination of this
sections.text:
The obligations set out in this Article shall subsist notwithstanding the termination of this
Agreement.
ARTICLE 16 - SEVERABILITY
If any provision or condition of this Agreement is prohibited or rendered invalid or unenforceable,
such prohibition, invalidity or unenforceability shall not affect the validity or enforceability of
any other provision of this Agreement which shall remain of full force and effect.
ARTICLE 17 - SURVIVAL
The termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason shall not release either Contracting
Party from any liabilities or obligations set forth in this Agreement which remain to be performed
or by their nature would be intended to be applicable following any such termination or expiration.
ARTICLE 18 - NON-WAIVER
A delay or failure by either Contracting Party to exercise or enforce any right under this
Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of that or any other right.
130 No. 12 of 2024 Kazungula Bridge Authority
ARTICLE 19 - ENTIRETY
This Agreement contains all covenants and stipulations agreed by the Contracting Parties. No
agent or representative of either Contracting Party has authority to make and the Contracting
Party shall not be bound or liable for, any statement, representation, promise or agreement not set
forth herein.
ARTICLE 20 - EXISTING TREATY, AGREEMENTS OR AMENDMENTS
In the absence of an agreement to the contrary, nothing in this Agreement shall affect the rights
and obligations of a Contracting Party arising from other agreements in force prior to the date this
Agreement comes into force for such a Contracting Party.
ARTICLE 21 - SIGNATURE
This Agreement shall be signed by duly authorised representatives of the Contracting Parties.
ARTICLE 22 - ENTRY INTO FORCE
This Agreement shall enter into force upon signature by both Contracting Parties.
ARTICLE 23 - AMENDMENTS
A Contracting Party may, in writing, propose an amendment to this Agreement and such
amendments shall take effect upon signature of such amendment by both Contracting Parties
or on a date to be mutually agreed in writing.
ARTICLE 24 - ASSIGNMENT
Neither Contracting Party may, without the prior written consent of the other Contracting
Party, assign a benefit or obligation imposed in this Agreement to a third party.
ARTICLE 25 - TERMINATION

sections.heading: Subject to Article 25 sub-article 2, this Agreement shall remain in force until terminated
sections.text:
Subject to Article 25 sub-article 2, this Agreement shall remain in force until terminated
by either Contracting Party.

sections.heading: This Agreement may be terminated by either Contracting Party giving one hundred and
sections.text:
This Agreement may be terminated by either Contracting Party giving one hundred and
eighty (180) days written notice to the other through diplomatic channels.

sections.heading: In the event that this Agreement is terminated, the Contracting Parties agree that outstanding
sections.text:
In the event that this Agreement is terminated, the Contracting Parties agree that outstanding
matters related to the operations of the Authority shall be settled amicably. In the event that
Contracting Parties fail to resolve the outstanding matters amicably, the matter shall be resolved
through arbitration as contemplated in Article 13.

sections.heading: In case of dissolution of the Authority, the Contracting Parties agree to share assets and
sections.text:
In case of dissolution of the Authority, the Contracting Parties agree to share assets and
cover any liabilities incurred by the Authority equally.
